etymology.description: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") refers to age or elderliness, and 年(年) (nián, "year") refers to the concept of time or age in years. Together, 老年 specifically denotes the period of life known as old age.
etymology.interpretation: 老(老) (lǎo, "old") indicates a stage in life characterized by experience and wisdom accumulated over time. 年(年) (nián, "year") brings in the temporal aspect, emphasizing the cumulative passage of years. Thus, 老年 represents the accumulation of years in life, focusing on the advanced age stage, often associated with retirement and reflection.

examples.chinese: 在老年(在老年)，保持健康是非常重要的(保持健康是非常重要的)。
examples.pinyin: Zài lǎonián, bǎochí jiànkāng shì fēicháng zhòngyào de.
examples.english: In old age, maintaining health is very important.

examples.chinese: 他的老年生活很幸福(他的老年生活很幸福)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de lǎonián shēnghuó hěn xìngfú.
examples.english: His old age is very happy.

examples.chinese: 人们常常在老年时回忆过去(人們常常在老年時回憶過去)。
examples.pinyin: Rénmen chángcháng zài lǎonián shí huíyì guòqù.
examples.english: People often recall the past in old age.
